metadata.summary: Python wrapper for the Zotero API

documentation:
[![Supported Python versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/Pyzotero.svg?style=flat)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/Pyzotero/) [![Docs](https://readthedocs.org/projects/pyzotero/badge/?version=latest)](http://pyzotero.readthedocs.org/en/latest/?badge=latest) [![PyPI Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/Pyzotero.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/Pyzotero) [![Anaconda-Server Badge](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/pyzotero/badges/version.svg)](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/pyzotero) [![Downloads](https://pepy.tech/badge/pyzotero)](https://pepy.tech/project/pyzotero)  

# Pyzotero: An API Client for the Zotero API

# Quickstart

1. `pip install pyzotero` **or** `conda config --add channels conda-forge && conda install pyzotero`
2. You'll need the ID of the personal or group library you want to access:
    - Your **personal library ID** is available [here](https://www.zotero.org/settings/keys), in the section `Your userID for use in API calls`
    - For **group libraries**, the ID can be found by opening the group's page: `https://www.zotero.org/groups/groupname`, and hovering over the `group settings` link. The ID is the integer after `/groups/`
3. You'll also need<sup>†</sup> to get an **API key** [here][2]
4. Are you accessing your own Zotero library? `library_type` is `'user'`
5. Are you accessing a shared group library? `library_type` is `'group'`.  

Then:

``` python
from pyzotero import zotero
zot = zotero.Zotero(library_id, library_type, api_key)
items = zot.top(limit=5)
# we've retrieved the latest five top-level items in our library
# we can print each item's item type and ID
for item in items:
    print('Item: %s | Key: %s' % (item['data']['itemType'], item['data']['key']))
```

# Documentation

Full documentation of available Pyzotero methods, code examples, and sample output is available on [Read The Docs][3].

# Installation

* Using [pip][10]: `pip install pyzotero` (it's available as a wheel, and is tested on Python 3.7 and up)
* Using Anaconda:`conda config --add channels conda-forge && conda install pyzotero`
* From a local clone, if you wish to install Pyzotero from a specific branch: 

Example:

``` bash
git clone git://github.com/urschrei/pyzotero.git
cd pyzotero
git checkout dev
pip install .
```

## Testing

Run `pytest .` from the top-level directory.

## Issues

The latest commits can be found on the [dev branch][9], although new features are currently rare. If you encounter an error, please open an issue.

## Pull Requests

Pull requests are welcomed. Please read the [contribution guidelines](CONTRIBUTING.md). In particular, please **base your PR on the `dev` branch**.

## Versioning

As of v1.0.0, Pyzotero is versioned according to [Semver](http://semver.org); version increments are performed as follows:  



1. MAJOR version will increment with incompatible API changes,
2. MINOR version will increment when functionality is added in a backwards-compatible manner, and
3. PATCH version will increment with backwards-compatible bug fixes.

# Citation

Pyzotero has a DOI:  
[![DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/1423403.svg)](https://zenodo.org/badge/latestdoi/1423403)  
You may also cite Pyzotero using [CITATION.cff](CITATION.cff).  
A sample citation (APA 6th edition) might look like:  
> Stephan Hügel, The Pyzotero Authors (2019, May 18). urschrei/pyzotero: Version v1.3.15. http://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.2917290

# License

Pyzotero is licensed under the [Blue Oak Model Licence 1.0.0][8]. See [LICENCE.md](LICENCE.md) for details.  

[1]: https://www.zotero.org/support/dev/web_api/v3/start
[2]: https://www.zotero.org/settings/keys/new
[3]: http://pyzotero.readthedocs.org/en/latest/
[7]: https://nose2.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
[8]: https://opensource.org/license/blue-oak-model-license
[9]: https://github.com/urschrei/pyzotero/tree/dev
[10]: http://www.pip-installer.org/en/latest/index.html
† This isn't strictly true: you only need an API key for personal libraries and non-public group libraries.

Define some exceptions

documentation: Raised if a parameter which is required isn't passed

documentation: Raised if the specified API call doesn't exist

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero_errors.UnsupportedParams
documentation: Raised when unsupported parameters are passed

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero_errors.UserNotAuthorised
documentation: Raised when the user is not allowed to retrieve the resource

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero_errors.TooManyItems
documentation: Raised when too many items are passed to a Write API method

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero_errors.MissingCredentials
documentation:
Raised when an attempt is made to create a Zotero instance
without providing both the user ID and the user key

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero_errors.InvalidItemFields
documentation: Raised when an attempt is made to create/update items w/invalid fields

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero_errors.ResourceNotFound
documentation: Raised when a resource (item, collection etc.) could not be found

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero_errors.HTTPError
documentation: Raised for miscellaneous URLLib errors

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero_errors.CouldNotReachURL
documentation: Raised when we can't reach a URL

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero_errors.Conflict
documentation: 409 - Raised when the target library is locked

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero_errors.PreConditionFailed
documentation:
412 - Raised when the provided X-Zotero-Write-Token has already been
submitted

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero_errors.RequestEntityTooLarge
documentation: 413 – The upload would exceed the storage quota of the library owner.

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero_errors.PreConditionRequired
documentation: 428 - Raised when If-Match or If-None-Match was not provided.

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero_errors.TooManyRequests
documentation:
429 - Raised when there are too many unfinished uploads.
Try again after the number of seconds specified in the Retry-After header.

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero_errors.FileDoesNotExist
documentation: Raised when a file path to be attached can't be opened (or doesn't exist)

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero_errors.TooManyRetries
documentation: Raise after the backoff period for new requests exceeds 32s

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero_errors.UploadError
documentation: Raise if the connection drops during upload or some other non-HTTP error code is returned

pylint: disable=R0904

functions.name: pyzotero.zotero.build_url
documentation:
Build a valid URL so we don't have to worry about string concatenation errors and
leading / trailing slashes etc

functions.name: pyzotero.zotero.token
documentation: Return a unique 32-char write-token

functions.name: pyzotero.zotero.cleanwrap
documentation: Wrapper for Zotero._cleanup

functions.name: pyzotero.zotero.chunks
documentation: Yield successive n-sized chunks from l.

functions.name: pyzotero.zotero.tcache
documentation: Take care of the URL building and caching for template functions

functions.name: pyzotero.zotero.backoff_check
documentation:
Perform backoff processing
func must return a Requests GET / POST / PUT / PATCH / DELETE etc
This is is intercepted: we first check for an active backoff
and wait if need be.
After the response is received, we do normal error checking
and set a new backoff if necessary, before returning

Use with functions that are intended to return True

functions.name: pyzotero.zotero.retrieve
documentation:
Decorator for Zotero read API methods; calls _retrieve_data() and passes
the result to the correct processor, based on a lookup

functions.name: pyzotero.zotero.ss_wrap
documentation: ensure that a SavedSearch object exists

functions.name: pyzotero.zotero.error_handler
documentation: Error handler for HTTP requests

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.__init__
documentation: Store Zotero credentials

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero._set_backoff
documentation:
Set a backoff
Spins up a timer in a background thread which resets the backoff logic
when it expires, then sets the time at which the backoff will expire.
The latter step is required so that other calls can check whether there's
an active backoff, because the threading.Timer method has no way
of returning a duration

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero._check_backoff
documentation:
Before an API call is made, we check whether there's an active backoff.
If there is, we check whether there's any time left on the backoff.
If there is, we sleep for the remainder before returning

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.default_headers
documentation: It's always OK to include these headers

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero._cache
documentation:
Add a retrieved template to the cache for 304 checking
accepts a dict and key name, adds the retrieval time, and adds both
to self.templates as a new dict using the specified key

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero._cleanup
documentation: Remove keys we added for internal use

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero._retrieve_data
documentation:
Retrieve Zotero items via the API
Combine endpoint and request to access the specific resource
Returns a JSON document

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero._extract_links
documentation: Extract self, first, next, last links from a request response

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero._updated
documentation:
Generic call to see if a template request returns 304
accepts:
- a string to combine with the API endpoint
- a dict of format values, in case they're required by 'url'
- a template name to check for
As per the API docs, a template less than 1 hour old is
assumed to be fresh, and will immediately return False if found

Add URL parameters
Also ensure that only valid format/content combinations are requested

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero._build_query
documentation:
Set request parameters. Will always add the user ID if it hasn't
been specifically set by an API method

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.set_fulltext
documentation:
"
Set full-text data for an item
<itemkey> should correspond to an existing attachment item.
payload should be a dict containing three keys:
'content': the full-text content and either
For text documents, 'indexedChars' and 'totalChars' OR
For PDFs, 'indexedPages' and 'totalPages'.

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.new_fulltext
documentation:
Retrieve list of full-text content items and versions which are newer
than <since>

Returns dict associating items keys (all no limit by default) to versions.
Accepts a since= parameter in kwargs to limit the data to those updated since since=

documentation:
Returns dict associating collection keys (all no limit by default) to versions.
Accepts a since= parameter in kwargs to limit the data to those updated since since=

The following methods are Write API calls

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero._attachment_template
documentation:
Return a new attachment template of the required type:
imported_file
imported_url
linked_file
linked_url

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero._attachment
documentation:
Create attachments
accepts a list of one or more attachment template dicts
and an optional parent Item ID. If this is specified,
attachments are created under this ID

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.show_operators
documentation: Show available saved search operators

documentation: Show available saved search conditions

Create a saved search. conditions is a list of dicts
containing search conditions and must contain the following str keys:
condition, operator, value

documentation:
Delete one or more saved searches by passing a list of one or more
unique search keys

documentation: Upload files to the already created (but never uploaded) attachments

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.add_tags
documentation:
Add one or more tags to a retrieved item,
then update it on the server
Accepts a dict, and one or more tags to add to it
Returns the updated item from the server

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.check_items
documentation:
Check that items to be created contain no invalid dict keys
Accepts a single argument: a list of one or more dicts
The retrieved fields are cached and re-used until a 304 call fails

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.item_types
documentation: Get all available item types

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.creator_fields
documentation: Get localised creator fields

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.item_type_fields
documentation: Get all valid fields for an item

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.item_creator_types
documentation: Get all available creator types for an item

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.item_fields
documentation: Get all available item fields

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.item_attachment_link_modes
documentation:
Get all available link mode types.
Note: No viable REST API route was found for this, so I tested and built a list from documentation found
here - https://www.zotero.org/support/dev/web_api/json

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.create_items
documentation:
Create new Zotero items
Accepts two arguments:
    a list containing one or more item dicts
    an optional parent item ID.
Note that this can also be used to update existing items

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.create_collection
documentation: Alias for create_collections to preserve backward compatibility

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.create_collections
documentation:
Create new Zotero collections
Accepts one argument, a list of dicts containing the following keys:

'name': the name of the collection
'parentCollection': OPTIONAL, the parent collection to which you wish to add this

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.update_collection
documentation:
Update a Zotero collection property such as 'name'
Accepts one argument, a dict containing collection data retrieved
using e.g. 'collections()'

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.attachment_simple
documentation:
Add attachments using filenames as title
Arguments:
One or more file paths to add as attachments:
An optional Item ID, which will create child attachments

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.attachment_both
documentation:
Add child attachments using title, filename
Arguments:
One or more lists or tuples containing title, file path
An optional Item ID, which will create child attachments

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.update_item
documentation:
Update an existing item
Accepts one argument, a dict containing Item data

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.update_items
documentation:
Update existing items
Accepts one argument, a list of dicts containing Item data

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.update_collections
documentation:
Update existing collections
Accepts one argument, a list of dicts containing Collection data

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.addto_collection
documentation:
Add one or more items to a collection
Accepts two arguments:
The collection ID, and an item dict

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.deletefrom_collection
documentation:
Delete an item from a collection
Accepts two arguments:
The collection ID, and and an item dict

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.delete_tags
documentation:
Delete a group of tags
pass in up to 50 tags, or use *[tags]

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.delete_item
documentation:
Delete Items from a Zotero library
Accepts a single argument:
    a dict containing item data
    OR a list of dicts containing item data

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zotero.delete_collection
documentation:
Delete a Collection from a Zotero library
Accepts a single argument:
    a dict containing item data
    OR a list of dicts containing item data

Zotero API methods
A full list of methods can be found here:
http://www.zotero.org/support/dev/server_api

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero.SavedSearch

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.SavedSearch._validate
documentation: Validate saved search conditions, raising an error if any contain invalid operators

Saved search functionality
See https://github.com/zotero/zotero/blob/master/chrome/content/zotero/xpcom/data/searchConditions.js

classes.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zupload

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zupload._verify
documentation:
ensure that all files to be attached exist
open()'s better than exists(), cos it avoids a race condition

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zupload._create_prelim
documentation: Step 0: Register intent to upload files

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zupload._get_auth
documentation: Step 1: get upload authorisation for a file

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zupload._upload_file
documentation:
Step 2: auth successful, and file not on server
zotero.org/support/dev/server_api/file_upload#a_full_upload

reg_key isn't used, but we need to pass it through to Step 3

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zupload._register_upload
documentation: Step 3: upload successful, so register it

methods.name: pyzotero.zotero.Zupload.upload
documentation:
File upload functionality

Goes through upload steps 0 - 3 (private class methods), and returns
a dict noting success, failure, or unchanged
(returning the payload entries with that property as a list for each status)

Zotero file attachment helper
Receives a Zotero instance, file(s) to upload, and optional parent ID
